data.title: As a developer, a dso:ui-designer agent creates design artifacts and returns structured results
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create a new dedicated sub-agent definition (dso:ui-designer) that handles design creation work currently performed by design-wireframe Phases 1-4 and 6. The agent accepts a story ID, produces design artifacts (spatial layout tree, SVG blueprint, token overlay, manifest), and returns structured results including design artifacts, cache status (CACHE_MISSING when UI discovery cache is stale/absent), and scope-split proposals.
**Why**: Design-wireframe is an 868-line skill invoked only by preplanning — converting it to a sub-agent eliminates the 3-level Skill nesting (sprint → preplanning → design-wireframe) that causes control flow failures.
**Scope**:
- IN: New agent definition file in plugins/dso/agents/; structured return payload schema (design artifacts, CACHE_MISSING status, scope-split proposals); extraction of design creation logic from current Phases 1-4, 6; Lite track support
- OUT: Design review logic (Phase 5 — moves to preplanning orchestration in a sibling story); changes to Playwright invocation method (handled by dependency epic 2f7a-7770); changes to ui-discover skill

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a dso:ui-designer agent definition exists in plugins/dso/agents/ and can be dispatched via the Agent tool to produce design artifacts for a given story ID
  ← Satisfies: "A new dso:ui-designer sub-agent handles design creation (current Phases 1-4, 6)"
- When this story is complete, the agent returns CACHE_MISSING with actionable instructions when the UI discovery cache is absent or stale
  ← Satisfies: "When the ui-designer agent detects a missing/stale UI discovery cache, it returns CACHE_MISSING with instructions"
- When this story is complete, scope-split proposals are included in the agent's return payload when the pragmatic scope splitter triggers
  ← Satisfies: "Scope-split proposals from the ui-designer agent bubble up in the return payload"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] New agent return payload schema must be thoroughly tested — malformed returns could silently break the design pipeline
- [Maintainability] Sequential execution with sibling stories essential — all modify closely related files
